raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
The business reported a net favourable variance of -£379 (-3.6%) against budget for February 2026, with full report actuals of £10,286 against a budget of £10,665. This headline position masks two material cost overruns — an approved pull-forward of the spring marketing campaign and an emergency HVAC repair — partially offset by a £1,200 nil spend on Subscriptions, the driver of which could not be confirmed from available sources and requires management review.

LINE COMMENTARY

Advertising & Marketing | +£667 (+66.7%) | Budget: £1,000 | Actual: £1,667
The £667 overspend reflects a deliberate pull-forward of the Q2 spring campaign from March into February, actioned at agency recommendation due to a weather-sensitive launch window. Actual spend of £1,667 was MD-approved in advance and aligns closely with the ~£1,600 commitment flagged in team communications at the time of the decision. A February campaign performance report confirms spend of £1,667 and notes that the March marketing budget has been reduced to nil to offset, leaving the full-quarter spend expected to remain on plan. No incremental budget exposure is indicated, though the March offset should be confirmed when March actuals are available.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-23T12:15:00Z - slack-advertising_&_marketing-broad - marketing-slack-2026-02-03: Campaign team — moving the Q2 spring campaign launch to this month. Client approval came in early, let's capitalise. Total Feb commitment will be ~£1,600 vs the £1,000 March plan. Approvals from MD signed off.
- Gmail - 2024-11-23T18:15:00Z - gmail-advertising_&_marketing-broad - gmail-2026-02-01-campaign-approval: MD approval for Q2 campaign acceleration. Spring campaign moved from March to February at agency recommendation — weather-sensitive launch window. Budget reforecast note attached.
- Calendar - 2024-11-23T22:15:00Z - calendar-advertising_&_marketing-broad - cal-2026-02-01: Marketing launch meeting — Spring campaign kick-off moved to 5 February. Agency briefed on 1 February per Slack decision.
- Gmail - 2024-11-23T18:45:00Z - gmail-advertising_&_marketing-narrow - gmail-2026-02-20-campaign-report: February campaign performance report. Spend £1,667. March budget reduced to £0 to offset. Net full-quarter spend on plan.

Repairs Maintenance | +£786 (+786.3%) | Budget: £100 | Actual: £886
The £786 overspend relates to an unplanned emergency HVAC failure in the server room on 14 February 2026. FastAir Engineering attended on an emergency callout basis; the invoice totalled £886.30 inclusive of parts and emergency rates, and was approved by the CFO on the same day. The spend is a one-off event with no recurring cost implication. Importantly, a building insurance claim has been submitted in connection with this repair; the outcome remains pending. Any recovery would reduce the net cost in a future period, but this is not yet confirmed and the full £886 remains recognised in February.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-19T12:15:00Z - slack-repairs_maintenance-broad - ops-slack-2026-02-14: URGENT — HVAC unit in server room failed overnight. Building manager called emergency engineer. Quote came in at £886. CFO approved. Works completed today.
- Gmail - 2024-11-19T18:15:00Z - gmail-repairs_maintenance-broad - gmail-2026-02-14-hvac-invoice: Invoice from FastAir Engineering — emergency HVAC repair 14 Feb 2026. £886.30 inclusive of parts and emergency callout rate. CFO approved same day.
- Calendar - 2024-11-19T22:15:00Z - calendar-repairs_maintenance-broad - cal-2026-02-14: Emergency maintenance — HVAC server room. Blocked out 08:00–18:00 BST for engineer access.
- Gmail - 2024-11-19T18:45:00Z - gmail-repairs_maintenance-narrow - gmail-2026-02-14-hvac-approval: CFO email approval for emergency repair. Building insurance claim submitted — outcome pending but expense recognised in February.

Subscriptions | -£1,200 (-100.0%) | Budget: £1,200 | Actual: £0
Nil spend was recorded against the £1,200 Subscriptions budget in February 2026. No supporting context was found across Slack or Gmail — broad and narrow searches on both channels returned no results. It is not possible to determine from available evidence whether this represents a cancellation, a billing timing issue, a supplier-side delay, or a deliberate decision to pause renewals. No context found — recommend review.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following line items showed variances below the significance threshold and were not subject to detailed investigation. They are noted for completeness.
- Sales | +£506 (+8.4%)
- Purchases | -£500 (-100.0%)
- Audit & Accountancy Fees | -£3 (-6.1%)
- Bank Fees | -£15 (-50.0%)
- Cleaning | -£1 (-0.8%)
- Entertainment - 0% | -£50 (-100.0%)
- Entertainment - 100% Business | +£13 (+0.0%)
- General Expenses | -£254 (-211.7%)
- Light Power Heating | +£13 (+14.9%)
- Motor Vehicle Expenses | -£71 (-54.6%)
- Postage Freight Courier | -£50 (-100.0%)
- Printing & Stationery | -£20 (-100.0%)
- Rent | -£46 (-4.4%)
- Telephone & Internet | -£6 (-13.3%)
- Travel National | -£150 (-100.0%)
